description: This document serves as a knowledge base artifact for the RFP automation system to ensure generated proposals comply with brand standards.

name: The product name 'TeleSphere' must never be abbreviated as 'NWT' or 'NWTECH' in
description: The product name 'TeleSphere' must never be abbreviated as 'NWT' or 'NWTECH' in client documents; 'NWTECH' is restricted to internal use only.

name: The registered office address for proposals must be listed as '12th Floor, Embas
description: The registered office address for proposals must be listed as '12th Floor, Embassy TechSquare, Outer Ring Road, Bengaluru – 560103' in all proposal documents.

name: The official website URL for all references must be 'www
description: The official website URL for all references must be 'www.nexwavetech.in' and no alternative URLs are permitted.

name: The color 'NexWave Red' (#E60000 / R:230 G:0 B:0) must be used exclusively for p
description: The color 'NexWave Red' (#E60000 / R:230 G:0 B:0) must be used exclusively for primary brand elements, headings, and call-to-action (CTA) buttons.

name: The color 'NexWave Navy' (#1A1A2E / R:26 G:26 B:46) must be used for body text a
description: The color 'NexWave Navy' (#1A1A2E / R:26 G:26 B:46) must be used for body text and dark background applications.

description: The color 'Pure White' (#FFFFFF / R:255 G:255 B:255) must be used for page backgrounds and clean space design elements.

description: The color 'Light Gray' (#F5F5F5 / R:245 G:245 B:245) must be used for table alternating rows and callout sections.

description: The color 'Mid Gray' (#888888 / R:136 G:136 B:136) must be used for footer text, captions, and metadata.

description: Light Red Tint (#FFF0F0) is used to highlight rows and totals.

description: Navy Light (#EEF2F7) is used for KV table labels and info panels.

description: Never use more than 3 colors on a single page.

description: Red is reserved for NexWave branding and may not be used for warnings unless combined with text.

name: Document Title (cover) must use Arial font, 52pt / Bold, NexWave Navy for the ma
description: Document Title (cover) must use Arial font, 52pt / Bold, NexWave Navy for the main title of the proposal.

description: Sub-Title (cover) must use Arial font, 26pt / Regular, NexWave Red for the descriptive subtitle.

description: Heading 1 must use Arial font, 28pt / Bold, NexWave Navy with a red bottom border.

description: Primary font for client-facing documents must be Arial.

description: Do NOT use Calibri, Times New Roman, or decorative fonts in client-facing documents.

name: Fallback font for client-facing documents is Helvetica Neue
description: Fallback font for client-facing documents is Helvetica Neue.

description: Page size for India market documents must be A4 (210mm x 297mm), not US Letter.

description: All proposal documents must have 1 inch (72pt / 1440 DXA) margins on all sides.

description: Content width for A4 pages with 1-inch margins must be ~9,026 DXA / 6.27 inches.

description: Body text must use Arial 20pt Regular with black (#000000) color.

description: Headings must use Arial 24pt Bold with NexWave Red color.

description: Table headers must use Arial 18pt Bold with white text on navy (#1A1A2E) background.

description: Footers/metadata must use Arial 16pt Regular with mid gray (#888888) color.

description: Technical/code elements must use Courier New 18pt Regular with black text.

description: Body text must use single line spacing (1.0) with 8pt spacing before and after paragraphs.

description: Body text must be left-aligned; cover titles must be center-aligned.

description: Table cell padding must be Top: 80–100 DXA, Bottom: 80–100 DXA, Left: 120 DXA, Right: 120 DXA.

description: Headers on every page must include 'Company name | Document title | Classification level'.

description: Client proposals must include 'Prepared by / Submitted to' lines in 20pt Mid Gray.

description: Client proposals must include a classification banner (CONFIDENTIAL / INTERNAL USE ONLY) in 18pt Bold, NexWave Red.

description: Table headers must have a NexWave Navy background (#1A1A2E), white text, and 18pt Bold formatting.

description: Table rows must alternate between white (#FFFFFF) and light gray (#F5F5F5) shading using ShadingType.CLEAR.

description: All table borders must be single line, 1pt, light gray (#DDDDDD).

description: Highlight/totals rows must use light red tint (#FFF0F0) with bold text.

description: Tables must specify both columnWidths (array) and individual cell widths (DXA) for consistent rendering.

description: Percentage widths must not be used in tables; only DXA values are allowed for cross-platform compatibility.

description: All RFP responses must include an Executive Summary section with 2 pages max, Vi-specific content, and quantified outcomes.

name: Proposals for India enterprise/telecom sector must be in English only; rural/gov
description: Proposals for India enterprise/telecom sector must be in English only; rural/government sector bids may be bilingual (Hindi + English).

name: All prices in India proposals must be in Indian Rupees (INR) with a forex protec
description: All prices in India proposals must be in Indian Rupees (INR) with a forex protection statement ('all prices in INR; no forex exposure for client').

name: Payment terms for India proposals must be Net 30 days from invoice, with a miles
description: Payment terms for India proposals must be Net 30 days from invoice, with a milestone-linked structure and bank guarantee for advance payment.

description: Client references must include a minimum of 3 references with contact details and note availability for site visits.

description: Implementation methodology must include a phase plan, named team, milestone commitments, and critical blackout periods.

description: Data migration strategy must use the TeleSphere Migration Factory with a zero-leakage commitment and parallel run plan.

description: Support and AMS model must include an SLA schedule, hypercare plan, and 5-year AMS structure.

name: Commercial proposals must reference NWTECH-PPC-2026-001 and include milestone pa
description: Commercial proposals must reference NWTECH-PPC-2026-001 and include milestone payments and TCO summary.

description: AWS Key Management Service (KMS) with CloudHSM must be used for key management.

name: TLS 1
description: TLS 1.3 must be the enforced minimum version for encryption in transit.

description: MFA must be implemented via Azure AD or Okta with SAML 2.0 / OAuth 2.0 / OIDC federation.
evidence: 2. RFP Security Requirements — Full Compliance Matrix

name: NexWave Technologies implements Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and Attribute-B
description: NexWave Technologies implements Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) integrated with CyberArk Privileged Access Management (PAM) and Open Policy Agent (OPA) policy engine.

name: Disaster Recovery (DR) operations must achieve RPO <15 minutes and RTO <1 hour
description: Disaster Recovery (DR) operations must achieve RPO <15 minutes and RTO <1 hour.

name: An active-active multi-region DR setup is implemented in Mumbai and Hyderabad
description: An active-active multi-region DR setup is implemented in Mumbai and Hyderabad.

name: Geo-failover is enabled via Route 53 for disaster recovery
description: Geo-failover is enabled via Route 53 for disaster recovery.

name: Disaster recovery tests are conducted every 6 months
description: Disaster recovery tests are conducted every 6 months.

name: TeleSphere supports DOT-mandated Lawful Intercept and Monitoring (LIM) requireme
description: TeleSphere supports DOT-mandated Lawful Intercept and Monitoring (LIM) requirements with CALEA-compatible architecture.

name: NexWave deploys Vi's BSS/OSS platform on AWS India (ap-south-1 Mumbai primary; a
description: NexWave deploys Vi's BSS/OSS platform on AWS India (ap-south-1 Mumbai primary; ap-south-2 Hyderabad DR).

name: AWS KMS and CloudHSM with FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSM are used for key management, wi
description: AWS KMS and CloudHSM with FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSM are used for key management, with customer-managed keys (CMK) available for Vi.

name: NexWave SOC (Bengaluru) must respond to P1 security events within 15 minutes and
description: NexWave SOC (Bengaluru) must respond to P1 security events within 15 minutes and achieve initial containment within 1 hour.

name: Critical (CVSS 9
description: Critical (CVSS 9.0+) penetration test findings must be remediated within 30 days of identification.

name: High-severity penetration test findings must be remediated within 60 days of ide
description: High-severity penetration test findings must be remediated within 60 days of identification.

name: NexWave operates a responsible disclosure program and tracks CVEs for all TeleSp
description: NexWave operates a responsible disclosure program and tracks CVEs for all TeleSphere components.

name: Recovery Point Objective (RPO) must be less than 15 minutes using AWS active-act
description: Recovery Point Objective (RPO) must be less than 15 minutes using AWS active-active replication with <5-second lag for transaction logs.

name: Recovery Time Objective (RTO) must be less than 1 hour via automated geo-failove
description: Recovery Time Objective (RTO) must be less than 1 hour via automated geo-failover using Route 53 health checks with no manual intervention.

name: Disaster recovery (DR) failover tests must be conducted every 6 months, with tes
description: Disaster recovery (DR) failover tests must be conducted every 6 months, with test reports provided to Vi's IT governance team.

name: A 24x7 Hypercare War Room must be maintained for 90 days post each phase go-live
description: A 24x7 Hypercare War Room must be maintained for 90 days post each phase go-live, co-located at Vi's Mumbai HQ and Bengaluru delivery center.

name: Professional Indemnity insurance coverage of INR 100 Crores per claim must be ma
description: Professional Indemnity insurance coverage of INR 100 Crores per claim must be maintained with New India Assurance Co. for FY2026-27, renewable annually.

name: Deliverables include software modules, configurations, documents, reports, data
description: Deliverables include software modules, configurations, documents, reports, data migration artifacts, or other outputs produced by the Vendor as specified in the applicable SOW.

name: Services include implementation, software deployment, data migration, training,
description: Services include implementation, software deployment, data migration, training, hypercare support, and Application Managed Services (AMS) as defined in the relevant SOW.

name: The TeleSphere Suite is NexWave's proprietary BSS/OSS software platform, includi
description: The TeleSphere Suite is NexWave's proprietary BSS/OSS software platform, including modules like OCS, Billing Engine, CRM, OMS, NIM, Revenue Assurance, Fraud Management, Self-Care, and SliceOrchestrator.

name: A phase of the platform is accepted as production-ready only after successful co
description: A phase of the platform is accepted as production-ready only after successful completion of acceptance testing criteria defined in the SOW.

name: The Vendor shall mobilize the programme team as specified in the SOW, including
description: The Vendor shall mobilize the programme team as specified in the SOW, including the named key personnel (Programme Director, Architecture Lead, and Domain Leads), from the contract effective date.

name: Named key personnel shall not be replaced without the Client's prior written con
description: Named key personnel shall not be replaced without the Client's prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld.

name: In the event of an unavoidable replacement of named key personnel, the Vendor sh
description: In the event of an unavoidable replacement of named key personnel, the Vendor shall provide a candidate with equivalent or superior qualifications for Client approval within 15 business days.

name: The Vendor commits to the milestone schedule defined in the applicable SOW
description: The Vendor commits to the milestone schedule defined in the applicable SOW.

name: The Vendor shall notify the Client in writing at least 20 business days in advan
description: The Vendor shall notify the Client in writing at least 20 business days in advance if it becomes aware that a milestone will not be met.

name: Unexcused delays beyond 30 days on critical path milestones shall trigger the de
description: Unexcused delays beyond 30 days on critical path milestones shall trigger the delay credits defined in Schedule 2 (SLA & Service Credits).

name: For all subscriber data migration activities, the Vendor guarantees zero billing
description: For all subscriber data migration activities, the Vendor guarantees zero billing disruption and zero revenue leakage as defined in the Migration Acceptance Criteria (Appendix to SOW).

name: Acceptance of data migration requires: (i) 100% subscriber record count reconcil
description: Acceptance of data migration requires: (i) 100% subscriber record count reconciliation, (ii) CDR value reconciliation within 0.01% tolerance, and (iii) parallel run sign-off by Client's billing team.

name: Any billing errors attributable to Vendor migration activities discovered within
description: Any billing errors attributable to Vendor migration activities discovered within 90 days of Go-Live shall be remedied by the Vendor at no additional cost.

name: The platform shall meet or exceed the performance benchmarks specified in Schedu
description: The platform shall meet or exceed the performance benchmarks specified in Schedule 1 (SLA Schedule) from the Go-Live Date.

name: Performance benchmarks include OCS rating latency (<50ms P99 at 1
description: Performance benchmarks include OCS rating latency (<50ms P99 at 1.5M TPS), availability (99.999% for OCS/Charging), bill run completion (10M accounts in <6 hours), and all other metrics in Schedule 1.

name: Failure to meet performance benchmarks triggers service credits per Schedule 2
description: Failure to meet performance benchmarks triggers service credits per Schedule 2.

name: Implementation payments are milestone-linked as specified in the Commercial Exhi
description: Implementation payments are milestone-linked as specified in the Commercial Exhibit.

name: AMS payments are monthly in arrears from the AMS Commencement Date
description: AMS payments are monthly in arrears from the AMS Commencement Date.

name: All amounts are exclusive of applicable GST (currently 18% on software and profe
description: All amounts are exclusive of applicable GST (currently 18% on software and professional services).

name: Invoices shall be raised by the Vendor within 5 business days of milestone achie
description: Invoices shall be raised by the Vendor within 5 business days of milestone achievement, accompanied by evidence of completion.

name: Client shall pay undisputed invoices within Net 30 days of receipt
description: Client shall pay undisputed invoices within Net 30 days of receipt.

name: For disputed invoices, the Client shall raise a written dispute within 15 busine
description: For disputed invoices, the Client shall raise a written dispute within 15 business days with specific grounds.

name: Both parties shall resolve disputes within 30 days of the dispute notice
description: Both parties shall resolve disputes within 30 days of the dispute notice.

name: Undisputed portions of disputed invoices shall be paid within the original Net 3
description: Undisputed portions of disputed invoices shall be paid within the original Net 30 timeline.

name: Overdue amounts beyond 45 days shall accrue interest at SBI MCLR (1-year) + 2% p
description: Overdue amounts beyond 45 days shall accrue interest at SBI MCLR (1-year) + 2% per annum, calculated daily.

name: Persistent non-payment beyond 90 days (with a 30-day cure notice) shall entitle
description: Persistent non-payment beyond 90 days (with a 30-day cure notice) shall entitle the Vendor to suspend non-critical services upon written notice.

name: AMS fees shall escalate annually at the lower of: (a) 4% per year (contractually
description: AMS fees shall escalate annually at the lower of: (a) 4% per year (contractually committed), or (b) the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) published by the Ministry of Commerce, Government of India.

name: The AMS price escalation cap is a contractual commitment and cannot be exceeded
description: The AMS price escalation cap is a contractual commitment and cannot be exceeded.

name: AMS price escalation applies from the second AMS year onward
description: AMS price escalation applies from the second AMS year onward.

name: All changes to agreed SOW scope, timeline, or commercial terms shall be governed
description: All changes to agreed SOW scope, timeline, or commercial terms shall be governed by the Change Control Process.

name: Either party may raise a Change Request ('CR') in writing using the standard CR
description: Either party may raise a Change Request ('CR') in writing using the standard CR form.

name: The Vendor shall provide a commercial and timeline impact assessment within 10 b
description: The Vendor shall provide a commercial and timeline impact assessment within 10 business days of a Change Request.

name: The Client shall approve, reject, or counter-propose a Change Request within 10
description: The Client shall approve, reject, or counter-propose a Change Request within 10 business days of receiving the impact assessment.

name: No change shall be implemented without a written Change Order signed by both par
description: No change shall be implemented without a written Change Order signed by both parties' authorized representatives.

name: Change Requests that reduce scope shall not entitle the Vendor to additional pay
description: Change Requests that reduce scope shall not entitle the Vendor to additional payment, but may reduce milestone-linked payments proportionally as agreed.

name: Service levels defined in the SLA schedule are material terms of the Agreement
description: Service levels defined in the SLA schedule are material terms of the Agreement.

name: The SLA schedule defines availability, latency, response, and resolution targets
description: The SLA schedule defines availability, latency, response, and resolution targets for each system component.

name: SLA measurement periods, exclusions (scheduled maintenance, force majeure, Clien
description: SLA measurement periods, exclusions (scheduled maintenance, force majeure, Client-caused outages), and credit calculation methodologies are defined in Schedule 1.

name: The OCS/Charging Engine has an SLA target of 99
description: The OCS/Charging Engine has an SLA target of 99.999% availability in a calendar month.

name: A credit trigger for the OCS/Charging Engine is <99
description: A credit trigger for the OCS/Charging Engine is <99.999% availability in a calendar month.

name: The monthly credit rate for the OCS/Charging Engine is 2% of the monthly AMS fee
description: The monthly credit rate for the OCS/Charging Engine is 2% of the monthly AMS fee per 0.001% shortfall in availability.

name: Termination for convenience during the implementation phase requires payment of
description: Termination for convenience during the implementation phase requires payment of all completed milestone payments.

name: Termination for convenience during the implementation phase requires payment of
description: Termination for convenience during the implementation phase requires payment of reasonable mobilization costs for work-in-progress (not to exceed 20% of remaining implementation CapEx).

name: Termination for convenience during the implementation phase requires payment of
description: Termination for convenience during the implementation phase requires payment of committed third-party costs that cannot be cancelled.

name: The Vendor shall provide exit assistance for up to 12 months after termination o
description: The Vendor shall provide exit assistance for up to 12 months after termination or expiry of the Agreement.

name: Exit assistance includes knowledge transfer to the Client's designated successor
description: Exit assistance includes knowledge transfer to the Client's designated successor vendor, data extraction in standard formats (CSV/JSON), platform documentation, and API specifications.

name: Exit assistance is charged at the T&M rates defined in the Commercial Exhibit
description: Exit assistance is charged at the T&M rates defined in the Commercial Exhibit.

name: The Vendor's obligation to maintain the platform continues during the exit assis
description: The Vendor's obligation to maintain the platform continues during the exit assistance period.

name: All services must be containerized using Docker/Helm and deployed on Kubernetes
description: All services must be containerized using Docker/Helm and deployed on Kubernetes (EKS) with infrastructure-as-code via Terraform.

name: The platform must use an event-driven microservices architecture with Apache Kaf
description: The platform must use an event-driven microservices architecture with Apache Kafka v3.6 as the central event bus, supporting 800K+ events/second.

name: The platform must maintain an active-active multi-region deployment with Mumbai
description: The platform must maintain an active-active multi-region deployment with Mumbai as primary and Hyderabad as DR site, including automated geo-failover and no single point of failure.

name: Horizontal scalability must be achieved via Kubernetes Horizontal Pod Autoscaler
description: Horizontal scalability must be achieved via Kubernetes Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA), tested to support 300M subscribers without re-architecture.

name: Observability must be implemented using Prometheus + Grafana for metrics, ELK st
description: Observability must be implemented using Prometheus + Grafana for metrics, ELK stack for log aggregation, Jaeger for distributed tracing, and Splunk SIEM for security.

description: TeleSphere OCS must support 280M subscribers and 1.5M TPS (as demonstrated in production at Stellar Mobile).

description: The system supports 3GPP Release 17 Ro/Gy/Rf interfaces (Ro for online charging, Gy for policy & charging, Rf for offline CDR) natively.

description: The system supports 5G SA Nchf interface for network slice charging, which is live at Crescent Telecom (Q1 2024) and supports production usage-based charging.

description: OCS rating latency must achieve P99 <50ms at 1.5M TPS, with <38ms P99 demonstrated in production.

name: The system guarantees peak throughput of 1
description: The system guarantees peak throughput of 1.5M TPS, with 2M TPS load-tested and 1.5M TPS production SLA contractually guaranteed.

name: The system scales to support up to 300M subscribers, with 280M live at Stellar M
description: The system scales to support up to 300M subscribers, with 280M live at Stellar Mobile and horizontal scaling tested to 300M without re-architecture.

name: The system supports prepaid real-time balance deduction, reservation, and reauth
description: The system supports prepaid real-time balance deduction, reservation, and reauthorization with <38ms P99 latency.

name: The company uses CDR-based mediation for convergent bill generation in postpaid
description: The company uses CDR-based mediation for convergent bill generation in postpaid and prepaid unified billing.

description: Enterprise contract billing must integrate with CPQ systems to support volume discounts and SLA-based credits for enterprise accounts.

description: OCS availability must meet 99.999% (Five-Nines) uptime as a contractual commitment.

name: The primary data store for OCS balance management is Apache Cassandra, ensuring
description: The primary data store for OCS balance management is Apache Cassandra, ensuring high-throughput and sub-millisecond read/write P99 performance.

description: Session state management for real-time session reservation and balance locks uses Redis Cluster.

name: CDR mediation processes data through Apache Kafka to a custom mediation engine,
description: CDR mediation processes data through Apache Kafka to a custom mediation engine, with PostgreSQL as the billing input store.

description: OCS and Billing systems must use separate data stores to avoid billing back-pressure during peak charging periods.

description: TeleSphere CRM v5.1 supports unified customer management for consumer prepaid, postpaid, SME, and enterprise segments.

description: The CRM provides a 360-degree view of customer interactions across call center, web, app, and retail POS channels.

description: CRM screen load time must be <1.4 seconds P99 in production, meeting the RFP target of <2 seconds.
